title: Defer typed error variants for new MCP tools to follow-up PR #96
context: PR #95 shipped four new MCP tools (critique, exemplars, project_style, decisions) that all returned IlluError::Other(String) for parse failures (14 call sites total). Axiom rust_quality_60_error_categories (Error Category Structure) flags this as the documented anti-pattern: callers cannot programmatically distinguish failure categories without parsing Display strings, and tests cannot use Error Path Specificity (axiom rust_quality_66_test_failure_variant) to assert which category fired. Tests in those modules used matches!(err, IlluError::Other(s) if s.contains("...")) which would silently pass after a refactor that flipped one failure cause into another.
decision: Ship PR #95 as-is and address the IlluError::Other saturation in follow-up PR #96. The follow-up introduces per-module typed error enums (ProjectStyleError, DecisionError, CritiqueInputError, ExemplarManifestError) wrapped by new IlluError variants via #[from], and tightens tests to use matches!(err, IlluError::ProjectStyle(ProjectStyleError::DuplicateLocalId(s)) if s == "project_dup") form. parse and parse_decision become module-local-error-typed (private fns) so internal callers and tests destructure the precise category without going through the IlluError wrapper; conversion to IlluError happens at the module boundary in load_from_path / load_from_dir / handle_critique / exemplars cache fn.

alternatives_considered.option: Block PR #95 until typed errors are in place
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: PR #95 is 88 commits across 5 phases (46 new axioms, 9 exemplars, 4 new MCP tools, max_tokens budget mode). Adding the error refactor would balloon scope and delay every user-visible feature. The error structure is a refactor that does not change behavior; it can land safely as a follow-up.

Surfacing axioms the implementation itself violates undermines the credibility of the corpus. The whole point of the dogfood is to act on what it surfaces. The escape-hatch doc on IlluError::Other already says 'prefer a domain variant when adding new sites'; this commits to that policy for the new tools.

alternatives_considered.option: Add IlluError::Json variants per-tool directly (no module-local enums)
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Would inflate IlluError to 30+ variants and couple every tool's failure taxonomy to the crate-wide error type. Per-module enums let each tool own its categories; the IlluError #[from] wrapper is one transparent variant per tool.

consequences: The four new tools' parse functions now return module-local error types directly (ProjectStyleError, DecisionError) or wrap typed enums into IlluError at their existing boundary (CritiqueInputError, ExemplarManifestError). serde_json::Error and io::Error are preserved in std::error::Error::source() chains rather than being stringified, satisfying the Error Source Chain axiom. Tests upgrade from substring/is_err checks to matches!(err, ProjectStyleError::DuplicateLocalId(_)) form. IlluError::Other(String) remains as the documented escape hatch for one-shot string-error sites elsewhere in the crate (200+ call sites untouched).
